title: A Quiet Place: Day One Crosses $100 Million Domestic Threshold
text: A Quiet Place: Day One has surpassed the $100 million mark at the domestic box office, reaching approximately $103 million as of July 11, 2024. The film achieved this feat within two weeks of its June 28 release date, demonstrating solid audience interest despite intense summer competition. The prequel launched with a $52.2 million opening weekend on a $67 million budget marking the franchise's strongest debut. This performance exceeded the original 2018 film's $50.2 million opening by roughly $2 million. The movie faced significant headwinds from major releases. Disney's Inside Out 2 dominated the charts during its opening weekend, while Despicable Me 4 subsequently competed for audience attention, making the achievement more noteworthy. Despite this progress, the prequel lags behind its predecessors. The original A Quiet Place earned $188 million domestically, while Part II reached $160 million. With lower critical reception (87% versus 96% and 91% on Rotten Tomatoes), Day One faces challenges reaching those totals.
